paper_metadata.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
paper_metadata.abstract: State-of-the-art computer vision systems are trained to predict a fixed set of predetermined object categories. This restricted form of supervision limits their generality and usability since additional labeled data is needed to specify any other visual concept. Learning directly from raw text about images is a promising alternative which leverages a much broader source of supervision. We demonstrate that the simple pre-training task of predicting which caption goes with which image is an efficient and scalable way to learn SOTA image representations from scratch on a dataset of 400 million (image, text) pairs collected from the internet. After pre-training, natural language is used to reference learned visual concepts (or describe new ones) enabling zero-shot transfer of the model to downstream tasks. We study the performance of this approach by benchmarking on over 30 different existing computer vision datasets, spanning tasks such as OCR, action recognition in videos, geo-localization, and many types of fine-grained object classification. The model transfers non-trivially to most tasks and is often competitive with a fully supervised baseline without the need for any dataset specific training. For instance, we match the accuracy of the original ResNet-50 on ImageNet zero-shot without needing to use any of the 1.28 million training examples it was trained on. We release our code and pre-trained model weights at https://github.com/OpenAI/CLIP.

extraction_data.innovation: CLIP introduces a contrastive pre-training task that jointly trains an image encoder and a text encoder to predict correct image-text pairings. This enables zero-shot transfer by using natural language descriptions to synthesize classifiers at test time.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Web-scale dataset of image-text pairs (not explicitly named, but implied from context: large-scale internet data)
extraction_data.extension: Uses a contrastive objective to align image and text embeddings in a shared multi-modal embedding space, maximizing cosine similarity for correct (image, text) pairs and minimizing it for incorrect ones.
extraction_data.research_problem: Zero-shot transfer learning for visual recognition using natural language supervision
extraction_data.application: Image classification, zero-shot identity detection, surveillance analysis, fine-grained object detection, gender and occupation bias analysis
extraction_data.paper_title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision

extraction_data.innovation: CLIP introduces a contrastive learning objective to align image and text representations using natural language supervision, enabling zero-shot transfer to image classification tasks without task-specific fine-tuning.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, zero-shot transfer, multimodal understanding, visual search
extraction_data.research_problem: Zero-shot image classification and transferable visual representation learning
extraction_data.extension: Uses a linear projection to map image and text representations to a shared multimodal embedding space, avoiding non-linear projections that may co-adapt with image-specific details.
extraction_data.paper_title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision

extraction_data.paper_title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision

extraction_data.innovation: CLIP introduces a contrastive learning framework that jointly trains vision and language encoders on massive web-scale image-text pairs, enabling zero-shot transfer to diverse downstream tasks by aligning visual and textual representations in a shared embedding space.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, zero-shot transfer, geo-localization, optical character recognition, facial emotion recognition, action recognition, fine-grained recognition (e.g., cars, traffic signs)
